---
title: "LRP60a: Unadjusted ITT on the SES complete-case subset (matched comparator to LRP60)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

If the model was not fitted in "reporting" mode, the results will be more approximate than where additional sampling has been performed.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import arviz as az
import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
trace = az.from_netcdf(_here / "trace.nc")
tau_summary = pd.read_csv(_here / "tau_summary.csv")
```

## Overview

LRP60a is the **matched comparator** that makes the LRP60 robustness check
interpretable. LRP60 adjusts the word-reading ITT for three SES / home-literacy
covariates (`mumedupost16`, `dadedupost16`, `agebooks`), but requesting them drops
every child with a missing value — so LRP60 runs on only ~33 of 54 children. A
naive LRP52-vs-LRP60 comparison therefore confounds the **SES adjustment** with the
**sample change** (the dropped children).

LRP60a breaks that confound: it is the **unadjusted** word-reading ITT fit on the
**exact same SES complete-case subset** as LRP60 (via `restrict_complete`, which
applies the complete-case mask *without* adding adjustment terms). It is identical
to LRP60 in every other respect, so the only difference from LRP60 is the
adjustment, and the only difference from LRP52 is the sample.

::: {.callout-note title="How to read the three fits"}

| Fit | Sample | Adjusts for SES | Isolates |
|---|---|---|---|
| LRP52 | full (n ≈ 54) | no | the headline effect |
| **LRP60a** | complete-case (n ≈ 33) | **no** | the dropped-children / selection effect |
| LRP60 | complete-case (n ≈ 33) | yes | the SES adjustment, sample held fixed |

**LRP60a vs LRP52** = how much the dropped children matter (selection).
**LRP60 vs LRP60a** = how much SES adjustment matters. The word-reading effect is
robust to SES adjustment **only if $\tau$ stays clearly beneficial from LRP60a to
LRP60**. See the dated three-fit comparison note for the verdict.

:::

## Model

Identical to LRP52 / LRP60 minus the SES adjusters. The linear predictor is

$$
\eta_i = \alpha
       + \tau\, G_i
       + \gamma_W\, \text{logit}_*(W_{\mathrm{pre},i}, N_W)
       + \sum_{k \in \{R,E,L,P,B,F,T\}} \gamma_k\, \text{logit}_*(k_{\mathrm{pre},i}, N_k),
$$

with the same Beta-Binomial word-reading likelihood,
$W_{\text{post},i} \sim \text{BetaBinomial}(N_W, \mu_i \kappa, (1-\mu_i)\kappa)$ and
$\mu_i = \sigma(\eta_i)$. No SES coefficients are present; the SES columns enter
*only* through the complete-case restriction. Sign convention as for the other ITT
models: **negative $\tau$ = intervention benefit**.

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Shared priors

![alpha](prior_alpha.svg){width=40%}
![tau](prior_tau.svg){width=40%}
![gamma_own](prior_gamma_own.svg){width=40%}
![gamma_cross](prior_gamma_cross.svg){width=40%}
![kappa](prior_kappa.svg){width=40%}

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Pair plot](pair_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Posterior](posterior_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Posterior predictive check

![Posterior predictive](posterior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## Treatment-effect summary

```{python}
# | echo: false
tau_summary
```

- `tau_logit_*`: posterior mean and $(1-\alpha)/2$, $1-(1-\alpha)/2$ quantiles of $\tau$ on the logit scale.
- `tau_prob_*`: implied marginal effect on the probability scale at the sample-mean baseline. Point-estimate sensitivity summary, not a causal contrast.
- `prob_tau_pos`: $P(\tau > 0)$.

## LOO-PSIS

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
